items.title: Police to review case of Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre

children.content: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

gallery.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera
WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch standing outside Parliament House in Perth in front of TV microphones.

thumbnail.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera
WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch standing outside Parliament House in Perth in front of TV microphones.

items.title: How Perth girl got caught in crossfire of 'deliberate policy of lethal force'

image.altText: Headshot of a father and his young daughter holding up a graduation certificate.

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

items.title: Bird flu prompts Papua New Guinea to ban Australian chicken and egg imports

children.content: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, suspends Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak, even though Australian authorities say there is no evidence the virus has spread.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Concerns raised about WA worker shortages amid vocational training drop

children.content: The number of West Australians enrolled in apprenticeships and traineeships is the lowest in five years after a post-COVID spike in numbers.

image.altText: Man with helmet climbs on scaffolding

items.title: Asteroid crater in Pilbara confirmed again as world's oldest

children.content: Researchers find a crater in WA's East Pilbara is 3 billion years old, but it has sparked debate in the scientific community.

gallery.altText: Rocky hills surrounding a flattish plain. The rocks and dirt are red. Small shrubs are scattered around.
Rock formations on the asteroid impact

thumbnail.altText: Rocky hills surrounding a flattish plain. The rocks and dirt are red. Small shrubs are scattered around.
Rock formations on the asteroid impact

items.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

articles.title: Socceroos should make 'harsh' call for final group game, Schwarzer says

children.content: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

image.altText: Patrick Beach in front of fellow Socceroos goalkeeper Mat Ryan at an Australia training session at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play Paraguay and what time is the game?

children.content: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Irankunda slides along a green pitch on his knees.

articles.title: Colombia scores late winner against Congo, England frustrated by resolute Ghana

children.content: The World Cup looks set for a second 0-0 draw before a late strike by Daniel Muñoz sees Colombia to a 1-0 win over DR Congo to sit atop Group K despite Portugal's 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan.

image.altText: Congo DR fan Michel Nkuka Mboladinga stands in among the crowd

gallery.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

thumbnail.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

items.title: Self-managed super sector blindsided by Labor–Greens deal on tax package

children.content: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

image.altText: Two suited men walk towards lecterns.

items.title: 'I love you': Sydney shark attack survivor's first words to family

children.content: A Sydney mother mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach almost a fortnight ago has spoken her first words after briefly waking from an induced coma.

image.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby

items.title: Qantas A380 to be inspected urgently after cracks found

children.content: Airbus says it will inspect 16 A380 planes, including one Qantas plane, after cracks were found in a key wing component.

image.altText: A Virgin and a Qantas airliner on a tarmac.

items.title: Bondi terror attack hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on father

children.content: Ahmed Al Ahmed has pleaded not guilty to allegations he assaulted and intimidated his father during an incident in March.

image.altText: Man in dark suit, white shirt looking down, another man with beard standing next to him

items.title: Wild birds tested for bird flu in Perth, Geraldton and South Australia

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

articles.title: 'More bad news' expected as testing widens after second WA bird flu case

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

image.altText: A man with beanie on holding a chicken with chickens on ground

articles.title: Poultry producers warn bird flu would be 'catastrophic for Tasmania'

children.content: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

image.altText: Brad stands in front of his farm gate, which has a biosecurity sign on it. He crosses his arms and looks at the camera.

articles.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

image.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

gallery.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

children.content: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

image.altText: A photo of Sue (Far right) with her family pictured behind them in Lebanon.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

image.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Limits for 'silent killer' chemical exposure on hold despite advice

children.content: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

image.altText: A worker in hi-vis clothing walks across a sandy construction site with machinery and Fiona Stanley Hospital in the background.

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A van carries people in the dark

gallery.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

thumbnail.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

items.title: 'I get a lot of anxiety': Borrowers caught out with high-interest car loans

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high-interest rates and fees.

image.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: Fourth WA council in turmoil after three councillors quit in three days

children.content: Three councillors have quit the City of South Perth in three days, citing concerns over a rates hike and broader dysfunction within the council.
